topic_title: Next-Generation AI Agents for Real-World Applications in the Apply AI sectors (RIA) (Partnership in AI, Data and Robotics)
destination: Achieving open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies
technology_readiness_level: Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
expected_outcomes: Significant improvements in the autonomy, robustness and reliability of AI agents through advanced planning mechanisms, memory management, and reasoning capabilities.
Innovative multi-agent frameworks and protocols demonstrating effective decentralized coordination and collaboration among multiple AI agents beyond the capabilities of individual agents.
scope_summary: Next-generation AI agents are autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models), that can plan, utilize tools and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions. Key aspects in designing effective AI agents include robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow agents to approach complex tasks by breaking them down into structured subgoals. Effective memory and state management are necessary to maintaining coherent long-term interactions, achieved through a balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions. Moreover, integrating external tools and APIs is essential for overcoming limitations in large AI models, enhancing agent performance in tasks requiring accuracy and reliability.
scope_research_areas: Enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization capabilities
Innovation in memory-augmented AI agents to facilitate robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning
Developing advanced multi-agent frameworks specifically tailored for collaborative agents, including research on AI agent frameworks based on mixed AI architectures
Advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities to enable real-world applications

eligibility_restrictions.eligible_entities: Legal entities established in EU Member States, Iceland, Norway, and associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
eligibility_restrictions.control_restrictions: Entities controlled by non-eligible countries may be excluded unless guarantees are provided.

procedure_exceptions: To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded to applications not only in order of ranking, but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of the three sectors (one in healthcare, one in advanced manufacturing and one in in-vehicle autonomous driving), subject to proposals passing all evaluation thresholds.
mandatory_cohesion_activities: European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA)
platform_requirements: Communicable results should be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform
Projects should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories and the Data Labs
Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs)
